basic_details.job_title: Central University of Kerala Teaching Recruitment 2026 - 48 Vacancies for Professor, Associate Professor & Assistant Professor
basic_details.short_information: Central University of Kerala (CUK) has released two official notifications dated 10 June 2026 inviting applications for 48 teaching posts across regular departments and the Integrated Teacher Education Programme (ITEP). Online applications open from 11 June 2026 at 4:00 PM and close on 10 July 2026 at 11:59 PM. The hard copy of the application must reach the university by 25 July 2026 at 5:00 PM. Eligible Indian citizens and Overseas Citizens of India may apply online through https://curec.samarth.ac.in.

application_fee.details: The online application fee for Central University of Kerala Teaching Recruitment 2026 is Rs. 2,000/-. Women candidates and candidates belonging to SC, ST, and PwBD categories are fully exempted from the application fee. The fee is non-refundable. | Payment must be made online through Credit Card, Debit Card, NEFT, or Net Banking. A copy of the transaction receipt should be enclosed along with the hard copy of the application.

qualification.details: Several conditions frequently lead to disqualification that candidates should be aware of. Degrees or diplomas obtained from institutions declared fake or derecognized by UGC at the time of obtaining the degree will render the candidate ineligible. The proof of peer-reviewed journal status, Impact Factor, and authorship claims must be supported by valid documents - scores will not be allotted without valid proof. Once appointed, faculty may be required to teach UG, PG, or other programmes and serve at any place/centre as notified by the university, in addition to regular teaching and research duties.

salary_details.details: The pay scales for Central University of Kerala teaching posts are fixed as per UGC/Government of India norms. Appointed candidates will be governed by the National Pension Scheme (NPS), and pay will be revised as per rules issued from time to time. | Note: The ITEP notification does not include a Professor-level post. Associate Professor in ITEP carries the same Academic Level 13A pay scale and Assistant Professor the same Academic Level 10 pay scale as the Non-ITEP posts.

selection_process: Central University of Kerala will shortlist candidates based on their qualifications and experience
and call them for a personal interview. Meeting the minimum prescribed qualifications alone does not guarantee an interview call - where the number of applications is large
the university may restrict candidates to a higher threshold of qualifications and experience at its discretion.

Scrutiny of online applications against prescribed eligibility criteria Shortlisting - based on qualifications and experience beyond the minimum (at university's discretion) Personal Interview - shortlisted candidates are notified by email only; call letters are issued through email only Document verification at the time of interview Medical fitness certificate from the university-specified medical board (if selected and appointed)
SC/ST/PwBD outstation candidates (residing beyond 30 km from the interview venue) who are called for interview will be reimbursed single second-class railway fare from station to station
on production of ticket and proof of category
as per Government of India rules. This concession does not apply to SC/ST/PwBD candidates already in Central or State Government services.

All correspondence from the university - including call letters - will be through email only. Candidates must keep their registered email ID active throughout the recruitment process. Queries may be directed to [email protected].
how_to_apply: This recruitment requires two mandatory steps - submitting the online application AND sending a hard copy by post. Completing only the online form is insufficient; applications where the hard copy is not received in time will be summarily rejected.
Visit the application portal: Go to https://curec.samarth.ac.in (CU Chayan Portal).
Register and fill the online application form for the specific post you are applying for. Note your Online Registration Number.
Upload supporting documents - SC/ST/OBC (Non-creamy layer)/PwBD certificates as applicable, educational qualification documents, experience certificates, and disability certificates (if applicable).
Pay the application fee online (Credit/Debit Card, NEFT, Net Banking) if applicable. Save the transaction receipt.

Candidates already in service must upload an Endorsement/NOC from their employer. If unable to upload at the time of application, upload an undertaking that the NOC/Endorsement will be submitted with the hard copy or at the time of interview. Failure to produce NOC at interview will disqualify the candidate.
Submit the online application before 10 July 2026 at 11:59 PM.

Prepare the hard copy: Print one copy of the completed application form. Attach self-attested copies of all documents claimed in the online application. Number all supporting documents serially; the serial numbers must correspond to the relevant entries in the hard copy.
Superscribe the envelope with: Name of Candidate | Online Registration No | Notification No | Post Applied for.

Dispatch the hard copy by post/courier to: The Recruitment Cell, Central University of Kerala, Tejaswini Hills, Periye, Kasaragod, Kerala – 671325. The hard copy must be received before 5:00 PM on 25 July 2026.
Candidates possessing foreign degrees must submit an Equivalence Certificate from the Association of Indian Universities (AIU) at the time of interview.

content_sections.heading: Central University of Kerala - Quick Summary
content_sections.text: Download Central University of Kerala Non-ITEP Notification PDF Download Central University of Kerala ITEP Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: Central University of Kerala - Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates must plan carefully, as this recruitment has two separate deadlines - one for the online application and another for the physical hard copy. Missing either will result in summary rejection. The eligibility and qualifications prescribed in the notification will be reckoned as on the closing date of online application, i.e., 10 July 2026 . Candidates are advised not to wait until the last date to avoid postal delay or technical failures - the university accepts no responsibility for delayed submissions.

rows.event: Last Date for Hard Copy Receipt at University

content_sections.heading: Central University of Kerala - Vacancy Breakdown
content_sections.text: Central University of Kerala is recruiting for 30 posts across regular departments under notification CUK/RC/10/TEACH/EN/2026. The posts include existing as well as anticipated vacancies. Professor (08 Posts) Associate Professor (07 Posts) Assistant Professor (15 Posts) The School of Education's Integrated Teacher Education Programme (ITEP) is recruiting 18 posts for teaching in the B.Sc-B.Ed, B.A-B.Ed, and B.Com-B.Ed integrated programmes under notification CUK/RC/11/TEACH/ITEP/EN/2026. Associate Professor (01 Post) Assistant Professor (17 Posts)

rows.subject: ITEP (Communicative Skills in English) for all programmes
ITEP (Communicative Skills in MIL-Hindi) for all programmes

content_sections.heading: Central University of Kerala Teaching Recruitment 2026 - Salary / Pay Scale
content_sections.text: The pay scales for Central University of Kerala teaching posts are fixed as per UGC/Government of India norms. Appointed candidates will be governed by the National Pension Scheme (NPS), and pay will be revised as per rules issued from time to time. Note: The ITEP notification does not include a Professor-level post. Associate Professor in ITEP carries the same Academic Level 13A pay scale and Assistant Professor the same Academic Level 10 pay scale as the Non-ITEP posts.

content_sections.heading: Central University of Kerala Teaching Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: For ITEP Posts , qualifications are as per the National Council for Teacher Education (Recognition, Norms and Procedure) Regulations, 2014 , and subsequent amendments. Essential qualifications: Master's Degree in Sciences or Mathematics with at least 55% marks (or equivalent grade in a point-scale) Master's Degree in Education with at least 55% marks (or equivalent grade) Doctor of Philosophy in Education Minimum 8 years of teaching and/or research experience at an academic/research position equivalent to Assistant Professor level, with a minimum of 7 publications in peer-reviewed or UGC-listed journals and a total research score of 75 as per Appendix II, Table 2 of UGC Regulations, 2018 Desirable: Diploma or Degree in Educational Administration or Leadership. The essential requirements common to all Liberal Discipline and Pedagogy posts are: (i) Post-Graduate degree in the concerned subject with at least 55% marks (or equivalent grade in a point-scale) - subject-specific requirements are listed below (ii) Bachelor of Education (B.Ed) Degree with at least 55% marks (or equivalent grade) (iii) National Eligibility Test or Doctor of Philosophy in Education or in the concerned subject, as prescribed by UGC for these categories of posts Subject-specific PG degree requirements for ITEP Assistant Professor posts: Desirable for Liberal Discipline and Pedagogy posts: (i) Master of Education or M.Ed with specialization; (ii) Ph.D in Education. (i) Post-Graduate degree in Education (M.Ed) with at least 55% marks (or equivalent grade) (ii) National Eligibility Test or Doctor of Philosophy in Education, or any other qualification prescribed by UGC for these categories of posts Desirable: Master's degree in Psychology, Philosophy, Sociology, or their allied subjects. A relaxation of 5% (from 55% to 50%) in qualifying marks at Graduate and Masters level is available to SC/ST/PwBD/OBC (Non-creamy layer) candidates for the purpose of eligibility and assessment of good academic record. This relaxation is applicable based only on qualifying marks, excluding any grace marks. A relaxation of 5% (from 55% to 50%) also applies to Ph.D. degree holders who obtained their Master's degree prior to 19 September 1991 . Where a university follows a grading system, a relevant grade regarded as equivalent to 55% by that university shall be considered valid. Candidates must submit a certificate of conversion from the degree-awarding university at the time of interview. The time taken to acquire M.Phil. and/or Ph.D. degrees will not be counted as teaching/research experience. Active service spent simultaneously pursuing a Research Degree while on teaching assignment without taking leave shall be counted as teaching experience. Offer letters, Appointment Orders, Joining Reports, and Relieving Orders will not be accepted as Experience Certificates. Experience certificates must clearly specify the break-up of service, scale of pay, and nature of appointment (Regular, Contract, Temp

rows.subject: B.A-B.Ed Political Science
rows.pg_degree_required: Post-Graduate in Political Science with min. 55%

rows.subject: B.Com-B.Ed Informatics Practice/Computer Science
rows.pg_degree_required: Post-Graduate in concerned/allied/relevant discipline with min. 55%

rows.subject: Communicative Skills in English (all programmes)
rows.pg_degree_required: Post-Graduate in English/Communicative English with min. 55%

content_sections.heading: Central University of Kerala Teaching Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee
content_sections.text: The online application fee for Central University of Kerala Teaching Recruitment 2026 is Rs. 2,000/- . Women candidates and candidates belonging to SC, ST, and PwBD categories are fully exempted from the application fee. The fee is non-refundable. Payment must be made online through Credit Card, Debit Card, NEFT, or Net Banking. A copy of the transaction receipt should be enclosed along with the hard copy of the application.

content_sections.heading: Reservation for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD)
content_sections.text: Two posts in this combined recruitment are specifically reserved for PwBD candidates. Only candidates with a minimum 40% disability are eligible to apply under PwBD reservation. Candidates whose disability falls under the 'likely to improve' or temporary disability category are not eligible for reservation; only progressive, non-progressive, or not-likely-to-improve conditions qualify. Candidates must upload the Disability Certificate in the prescribed format as per Rule 18(1) of Rights of Persons with Disabilities Rules, 2017 (Form V, Form VI, or Form VII as applicable), issued by the Competent Authority, and must forward a copy of the certificate along with the hard copy of the application.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: This recruitment requires two mandatory steps - submitting the online application AND sending a hard copy by post. Completing only the online form is insufficient; applications where the hard copy is not received in time will be summarily rejected. Visit the application portal: Go to https://curec.samarth.ac.in (CU Chayan Portal). Register and fill the online application form for the specific post you are applying for. Note your Online Registration Number. Upload supporting documents - SC/ST/OBC (Non-creamy layer)/PwBD certificates as applicable, educational qualification documents, experience certificates, and disability certificates (if applicable). Pay the application fee online (Credit/Debit Card, NEFT, Net Banking) if applicable. Save the transaction receipt. Candidates already in service must upload an Endorsement/NOC from their employer. If unable to upload at the time of application, upload an undertaking that the NOC/Endorsement will be submitted with the hard copy or at the time of interview. Failure to produce NOC at interview will disqualify the candidate. Submit the online application before 10 July 2026 at 11:59 PM . Prepare the hard copy: Print one copy of the completed application form. Attach self-attested copies of all documents claimed in the online application. Number all supporting documents serially; the serial numbers must correspond to the relevant entries in the hard copy. Superscribe the envelope with: Name of Candidate | Online Registration No | Notification No | Post Applied for. Dispatch the hard copy by post/courier to: The Recruitment Cell, Central University of Kerala, Tejaswini Hills, Periye, Kasaragod, Kerala – 671325. The hard copy must be received before 5:00 PM on 25 July 2026 . Candidates possessing foreign degrees must submit an Equivalence Certificate from the Association of Indian Universities (AIU) at the time of interview.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Central University of Kerala will shortlist candidates based on their qualifications and experience, and call them for a personal interview. Meeting the minimum prescribed qualifications alone does not guarantee an interview call - where the number of applications is large, the university may restrict candidates to a higher threshold of qualifications and experience at its discretion. The selection stages are: Scrutiny of online applications against prescribed eligibility criteria Shortlisting - based on qualifications and experience beyond the minimum (at university's discretion) Personal Interview - shortlisted candidates are notified by email only; call letters are issued through email only Document verification at the time of interview Medical fitness certificate from the university-specified medical board (if selected and appointed) SC/ST/PwBD outstation candidates (residing beyond 30 km from the interview venue) who are called for interview will be reimbursed single second-class railway fare from station to station, on production of ticket and proof of category, as per Government of India rules. This concession does not apply to SC/ST/PwBD candidates already in Central or State Government services. All correspondence from the university - including call letters - will be through email only . Candidates must keep their registered email ID active throughout the recruitment process. Queries may be directed to [email protected] .

content_sections.heading: Important Notes on Eligibility and Documents
content_sections.text: Several conditions frequently lead to disqualification that candidates should be aware of. Degrees or diplomas obtained from institutions declared fake or derecognized by UGC at the time of obtaining the degree will render the candidate ineligible. The proof of peer-reviewed journal status, Impact Factor, and authorship claims must be supported by valid documents - scores will not be allotted without valid proof. Once appointed, faculty may be required to teach UG, PG, or other programmes and serve at any place/centre as notified by the university, in addition to regular teaching and research duties.

content_sections.heading: Troubleshooting and Common Mistakes to Avoid
content_sections.text: Even experienced applicants make avoidable errors in central university recruitment. Here are the most common pitfalls: Missing the hard copy deadline is the most frequent cause of rejection. The online form and the hard copy are both mandatory. Many candidates submit the online form on the last day and fail to dispatch the hard copy in time for it to be received by 25 July 2026 at 5:00 PM. Post or courier well in advance. Incorrect serial numbering of documents causes processing delays. Every document enclosed with the hard copy must be serially numbered, and those numbers must be cross-referenced against the relevant entries in the application form. Experience certificates that don't meet UGC Clause 10 requirements are a very common issue for this type of recruitment. Offer letters, appointment orders, joining reports, and relieving orders are explicitly not accepted. Ensure your experience certificate specifies: period of service, scale of pay, nature of appointment (Regular/Contract/Temporary), and nature of the organisation. Envelope not superscribed correctly can lead to misrouting. The envelope must clearly mention the candidate's name, Online Registration Number, Notification Number (from page 2 against each post), and Post Applied for. Uploading a NOC from the employer is mandatory for candidates in service. If the NOC is not available at the time of online submission, upload a signed undertaking stating it will be submitted with the hard copy - but do not leave it until the interview, as candidates without NOC will not be permitted to attend. Grading system candidates must submit a conversion certificate from their university. Stating CGPA without an official conversion document is not accepted; percentages must be mentioned in the online form calculated as per university norms.

content_sections.heading: Central University of Kerala Teaching Recruitment 2026 - Important Links

rows.link_address: The Recruitment Cell, Central University of Kerala, Tejaswini Hills, Periye, Kasaragod, Kerala – 671325

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply online for Central University of Kerala Teaching Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is 10 July 2026 at 11:59 PM. However, candidates must also ensure the hard copy of the application reaches the Recruitment Cell, Central University of Kerala, Kasaragod, by 25 July 2026 at 5:00 PM.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in total in CUK Teaching Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 48 teaching vacancies in total — 30 posts under the regular department notification (Non-ITEP) and 18 posts under the ITEP (School of Education) notification. Posts are at Professor, Associate Professor, and Assistant Professor levels.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application fee for CUK Teaching Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application fee is Rs. 2,000/-. Women candidates and candidates belonging to SC, ST, and PwBD categories are fully exempted from the fee. Payment is accepted online through Credit/Debit Card, NEFT, or Net Banking. The fee is non-refundable.

faq.question: Q4. Is the hard copy submission mandatory?
faq.answer: Yes, submission of a hard copy is mandatory for both notifications. Applications will be summarily rejected if the hard copy with all supporting documents is not received at the Recruitment Cell before 5:00 PM on 25 July 2026.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary for Professor at Central University of Kerala?
faq.answer: The Professor post carries Academic Level 14 with a pay scale of Rs. 1,44,200 to Rs. 2,18,200. Associate Professor is at Academic Level 13A (Rs. 1,31,400 – 2,17,100) and Assistant Professor at Academic Level 10 (Rs. 57,700 – 1,82,400).

faq.question: Q6. What is the minimum marks requirement for eligibility?
faq.answer: The essential qualification for most posts requires a minimum of 55% marks (or equivalent grade in a point-scale) at the Master's level. A relaxation of 5% (to 50%) is available for SC/ST/PwBD/OBC (Non-creamy layer) candidates. Ph.D. holders who completed their Master's degree before 19 September 1991 also get this 5% relaxation.

faq.question: Q7. What qualifications are needed for ITEP Assistant Professor posts?
faq.answer: ITEP Assistant Professor candidates need: (i) a PG degree in the relevant subject with at least 55%; (ii) a B.Ed degree with at least 55%; and (iii) either NET qualification or a Ph.D in Education or the concerned subject. For Educational Studies posts, an M.Ed with at least 55% plus NET or Ph.D is required.

faq.question: Q8. Where do I apply for CUK Teaching Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online at https://curec.samarth.ac.in (the CU Chayan Portal). No other mode of application is accepted. After completing the online form, the hard copy must be posted to the Recruitment Cell, Central University of Kerala, Tejaswini Hills, Periye, Kasaragod, Kerala – 671325.

faq.question: Q9. Is there an age limit for CUK Teaching Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The notification does not specify any age limit for these teaching posts.

faq.question: Q10. Are candidates from previous cancelled advertisements required to reapply?
faq.answer: Yes. A previous advertisement (CUK/EST/RECRT/TEACH/2023 dated 05 June 2023) for Professor (04), Associate Professor (02) and Assistant Professor (04) posts has been cancelled on administrative grounds. Candidates who had applied against that advertisement and wish to be considered for the fresh posts must apply afresh. However, they need not pay the application fee again if they had already paid for the cancelled advertisement.
